  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'string'.
tluaLibLuaJIT.tlua(31,13): error TS2554: Expected 1 arguments, but got 2.
tluaLibLuaJIT.tlua(32,20): error TS2554: Expected 2 arguments, but got 3.
tluaLibLuaJIT.tlua(33,7): error TS2741: Property '__luaUserdataBrand' is missing in type 'LuaThread' but required in type 'LuaUserdata'.


==== tluaLibLuaJIT.tlua (5 errors) ====
    local bitlib = require("bit");
    local ffiLib = require("ffi");
    local jitlib = require("jit");
    local profile = require("jit.profile");
    local newTable = require("table.new");
    local buffer = require("string.buffer");
    
    local masked: number = bitlib.band(0xff, 0x0f);
    local platform: string = jitlib.os;
    local ctype: LuaCType = ffiLib.typeof("int[?]", 4);
    local memory: LuaCData = ffiLib.new(ctype);
    local argumentsObject: IArguments;
    local constructorFunction: NewableFunction;
    local presized: object = newTable(8, 4);
    local encoded: string = buffer.encode(presized);
    local randomValue: number = math.random(1, 10);
    local date: LuaDateTable = os.date("*t");
    local fileKind: "file" | "closed file" | nil = io.type(io.stdout);
    local asserted, message = assert(1, "ok");
    local assertedValue: number = asserted;
    local assertionMessage: string = message;
    profile.start("f", function(thread, samples, vmstate)
        print(thread, samples, vmstate);
    end);
    local first, second = string.find("typed lua", "lua");
    local ok: number = assert(first);
    local finish: number = assert(second);
    
    bitlib.band("wrong", 1);
                ~~~~~~~
!!! error TS2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
    local wrong: string = ffiLib.sizeof(memory);
          ~~~~~

    math.log(8, 2);

    string.rep("a", 2, ",");
